title: One Battle After Another Wins Best Picture and Best Editing at 2026 Oscars
text: Paul Thomas Anderson's film One Battle After Another claimed the Best Picture award at the 98th Academy Awards ceremony held March 15, 2026, at Hollywood's Dolby Theatre. The Warner Bros. production also won Best Film Editing, awarded to Andy Jurgensen. The film's total haul reached six Oscars during the evening, with Anderson personally winning trophies for Best Director and Best Adapted Screenplay. Sean Penn earned Best Supporting Actor honors, while Cassandra Kulukundis won the inaugural award for Best Casting. Upon accepting the top prize, Anderson remarked: What a night. He concluded his speech by inviting colleagues to celebrate, saying Let's have a martini. Adapted from Thomas Pynchon's 1990 novel Vineland, the film follows an ex-revolutionary forced back into conflict while protecting his daughter. Anderson's win marked a significant achievement after previously receiving 11 nominations without winning across films including Boogie Nights, Magnolia, and There Will Be Blood.
